attributes.action: Stop UK Government and Departments from labelling "State Pension" as benefits!
attributes.additional_details:
As State Pension has usually been contributed to over many years/decades, it seems wrong to call it a "benefit", and should revert back to what it has always been called, i.e. State Pension.
I strongly object to the inference that I receive a "benefit", when I paid into the system for over 50 years!
If we can get 100,000 signatures we can get our work shy MP’s to debate this in Parliament.
attributes.background:
I want the U.K. Government to debate this issue in Parliament and re-classify this as a government scheme that’s a right as we have paid into it as WORKERS!
As a benefit, they are free to reduce or end it when the proceeds can go towards Working Age Benefits which is growing exponentially.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign this petition which calls for a similar action: 
Stop defining state pensions as a benefit https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/747342
